description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who has been asked to perform a realistic “test-restore” using only standard GNU/Linux command–line utilities – most notably find(1) and xargs(1).

  Existing data:
    • All source backup material already lives below
        /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/
      and is organised in date-stamped sub-directories such as
        …/2023-07-14/files/
        …/2023-07-15/files/
      Each “files” directory contains a mixture of “*.bak” files and a few
      ordinary files.  ONLY the “*.bak” files are to be treated as restorable
      backups.

    • A write-able empty directory already exists at
        /home/user/restore_test/
      This job must create everything else you need under that directory.

  Goal of the job:
    1. Use a single find→xargs pipeline (you may insert other small utilities
       into the pipeline if you wish) to locate every regular file whose name
       ends in “.bak” anywhere below
          /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups
    2. For every match, copy the file into
          /home/user/restore_test/restored/
       using exactly the same sub-directory structure as the source, but
       REMOVE the trailing “.bak” suffix from the destination filename.
       Example:
         Source: /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg.bak
         Destination: /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg
    3. Produce a plain-text log file named
          /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
       that documents the action taken for each restored file.  The format of
       every data line in the log MUST be:

          <absolute_source_path> -> <absolute_destination_path> [OK]

       • The two paths are absolute and separated by space, hyphen-greater “->”,
         another space, then “[OK]”.
       • There is exactly one space before and after “->”.
       • “[OK]” must be in all caps, enclosed in square brackets.
       • No extra whitespace is allowed at either end of the line.

    4. When all per-file lines are written, append ONE summary line:

          TOTAL: N files restored

       • N is the decimal count of lines written in step 3.
       • The word TOTAL is all caps; there is a colon and a single space before
         the number; “files restored” must be written exactly as shown.

    5. Sort the per-file lines in /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
       lexicographically (byte order) BEFORE the final TOTAL line is added.

  Verification notes (VERY IMPORTANT):
    • The automated test will:
        – Check that every “*.bak” file that originally existed has a
          corresponding restored copy without the “.bak” suffix inside
          /home/user/restore_test/restored/…  (directory names must match)
        – Confirm that the restore_report.log file exists and matches the
          exact line format, order, and TOTAL line described above.
        – Confirm that the numerical value in the TOTAL line matches the count
          of “[OK]” lines directly above it.
    • File contents must be identical between every source “.bak” and its
      corresponding restored copy.

  Constraints & hints:
    • You have standard user (non-root) permissions only.
    • Do not hard-code the list of filenames; the exercise must be solved with
      find and xargs (or xargs-compatible alternatives such as parallel) so it
      keeps working even if more “.bak” files appear.
    • Think about directories: you may have to create them before copying.
    • Remember that find’s “-print0” in combination with “xargs -0” prevents
      trouble with spaces or other special characters in filenames.

  When you are finished, the ONLY things that should be new or modified under
  /home/user are:
    • The directory tree /home/user/restore_test/restored/… (created by you)
    • The file        /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and files BEFORE the task starts
  (all files are readable by the user unless otherwise specified):

  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg.bak
      Content: "server=alpha\nport=8080\n"
  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/data1.sql.bak
      Content: "CREATE TABLE test1 (id INTEGER);\n"
  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/notes.txt.bak
      Content: "Backup note for July 14\n"
  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/random.tmp
      Content: binary blob (ignored by tests)

  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-15/files/config2.cfg.bak
      Content: "server=beta\nport=9090\n"
  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-15/files/data2.sql.bak
      Content: "CREATE TABLE test2 (id INTEGER);\n"
  /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-15/files/readme.md
      Content: "This is a readme."  (ignored)

  Empty working directory provided:
      /home/user/restore_test/    (exists and is empty)

  Expected state AFTER successful completion:

  1. New directory hierarchy created:
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-15/files/

  2. Restored files (contents must be byte-identical to source, but filename
     has no .bak suffix):
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/data1.sql
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/notes.txt
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-15/files/config2.cfg
     /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-15/files/data2.sql

  3. RESTORE LOG
     File: /home/user/restore_test/restore_report.log
     Exact content (including final newline on every line):

     /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg.bak -> /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/config1.cfg [OK]
     /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/data1.sql.bak -> /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/data1.sql [OK]
     /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-14/files/notes.txt.bak -> /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-14/files/notes.txt [OK]
     /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-15/files/config2.cfg.bak -> /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-15/files/config2.cfg [OK]
     /home/user/backup_repo/daily_backups/2023-07-15/files/data2.sql.bak -> /home/user/restore_test/restored/2023-07-15/files/data2.sql [OK]
     TOTAL: 5 files restored

  Notes for the automated verifier:
    • It should ignore any files in the backup tree that do NOT end in “.bak”.
    • It must ensure that “restored” filenames no longer contain “.bak”.
    • It must fail if the restore_report.log deviates character-for-character
      from the template shown above, including order, spacing and newlines.
